text: A light source is introduced through a ten-millimeter port in the right iliac fossa, with the light cable wrapped around the rectum. The abdomen is desufflated while the light remains in place, allowing for illumination control during the procedure.

The transanal part of the procedure begins with the closure of the rectal lumen using a purse string suture. The light source from the abdomen is visible through the tissue, providing transillumination that enhances the view and facilitates safe dissection.
